# Questions in Livestream 112

Live Stream #112: Questions Are The Reward

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:06</a> | Was Mordecai made a eunuch when he entered Ahasuerus' service in Esther 8:9? | He said no; Mordecai was given a one-off commission to write a decree in the king's name, not made a permanent palace official. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=887" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:47</a> | How widespread were royal signet rings in the Persian world? | He said they were tightly controlled, with essentially one true royal signet typically handled by a seal-bearer rather than widely dispersed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=966" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:06</a> | Were Persian gallows a crucifixion rather than a noose? | He said no; Persian gallows meant impalement on a sharpened pole rather than hanging by the neck. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=1061" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:41</a> | How did Hatshepsut prove military skill as pharaoh? | He said she largely did not; she stayed out of campaigns and delegated war-making to generals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=1206" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:06</a> | Are people forbidden from developing biotechnology that could make humans immortal? | He distinguished life extension from immortality and said making humans unable to die would be extremely unwise even if not stated as a simple prohibition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=1475" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:35</a> | What was the state of the Egyptian military under Thutmose III? | He described it as highly trained, well equipped, and arguably the most capable army Egypt ever fielded. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=1612" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:52</a> | In Judges 1:16, why did Moses' in-laws go from Jericho, the city of palms, if Joshua 6:26 cursed anyone rebuilding Jericho? | He said the city of palms was a small village, not the rebuilt fortified Jericho; the curse is fulfilled later in 1 Kings 16:34. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=1940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:20</a> | If Merneptah attacked Israel in the 1100s, could that be a misspeaking of the century? | He said it was probably a misspeaking, but still thought the broader historical argument needed work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=2221" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:01</a> | What does he think of alternative theories for the origin of Yahweh? | He rejected the moon-god theory and said he finds a Midian-linked origin more plausible than the view that explicit Yahweh worship was already present in Avaris. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=2431" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:31</a> | Did Zoroastrianism influence early Judaism? | He said not the earliest forms; later prophetic or postexilic material may show limited Persian influence, but he thinks the influence is often overstated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=2579" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:59</a> | Does Irenaeus' statement about the church being caught up support a pre-tribulation rapture? | He said no; he read it as premillennial rather than pre-tribulational and called the pre-trib reading anachronistic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=2777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:17</a> | Is Jabin in Judges 4 the same Jabin as in Joshua 11? | He said no, and suggested the later `Hazor` title may reflect dynastic or relocated continuity rather than the same ruler. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=3009" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:09</a> | Why did Egyptians give Ramesses III a hard time economically even though he helped defend Egypt from the Sea Peoples? | He said rampant inflation and loss of imperial revenue damaged daily life, so military success did not preserve popularity decades later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=3229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:49</a> | Were the Sea Peoples one people or several, and were they Cretan or Greek? | He said they were several Indo-European groups, likely tied to the Greek archipelago and western Anatolia, including groups later associated with the Philistines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=3405" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:45</a> | Why do some scholars place the Exodus during the reign of Ramesses III? | He said they usually do so from theories about late Pentateuchal or Hebrew development rather than from the Egyptian archaeological evidence itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=3643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:43</a> | What are his thoughts on Genesis 4:26, and was Yahweh worshiped in other cultures besides the patriarchs and Hebrews? | He read the verse as retrojective language about calling on God, and said some southern Levantine groups may have worshiped Yahweh or related divine names. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=3889" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:49</a> | Which Egyptian archaeological dig interests him most now, and which biblically linked dig interests him most? | He mentioned Pithom, Wadi Tumilat, Heliopolis, and Hazor as especially interesting because of their potential biblical or background relevance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=4072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:52</a> | What is his most controversial theological position, stated provocatively? | He said his Exodus date is his most controversial position and has cost him academic opportunities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=4185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:45</a> | When the Israelites left Egypt, would they have used the Egyptian calendar, and do calendar differences make biblical dates harder to pinpoint? | He said the early Hebrew calendar was Canaanite rather than Egyptian, later calendars reflected hegemony, and changing systems can complicate date calculations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=4463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:23</a> | How does the evidence suggest Akhenaten died? | He said the evidence is unclear, but the unfinished burial goods suggest a sudden death and an awkward transition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=4690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:10</a> | Is he going to ETS this year? | He said yes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=4722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:42</a> | Does he see Jewish roots to the sacraments? | He said yes, especially communion in Passover and baptism in Jewish ritual washing and initiation practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=4925" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:05</a> | Did some Second Temple Jews believe they were eating the same Passover as Moses and everyone else? | He said he had not seen that in the texts and would defer to a specialist in Second Temple Judaism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=5090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:50</a> | How much autonomy did Egyptian scribes have in what they wrote? | He said very little. Most scribes were bureaucratic clerks and copyists doing assigned work rather than exercising academic freedom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=5222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:02</a> | Why did Thutmose III deface only Hatshepsut's public monuments rather than private material too? | He said the goal was to destroy her public image, so private or tomb contexts were less relevant and sometimes sacrosanct. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=5380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:40</a> | Was Moses' Cushite wife in Numbers 12 Zipporah or someone else, and why was Miriam punished? | He said she was someone else; the issue involved tribal alliance and prophetic authority, and Miriam was punished for entering the tent of meeting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=5676" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:36</a> | Did ancient Egyptians have riots before the first recorded workers' strike? | He said there are no records of earlier riots, though food insecurity could have produced unrest before the first attested strike. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=5743" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:43</a> | Were the prophecies against Tyre fulfilled even though Alexander, not Nebuchadnezzar, is often associated with Tyre's destruction? | He said there is evidence Nebuchadnezzar did attack or control Tyre, and Alexander can be part of the broader many-nations fulfillment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=5869" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:49</a> | Why was there democratization of afterlife material in Egypt, with texts spreading beyond royalty? | He said the process began earlier than the Second Intermediate Period, from late Old Kingdom and Middle Kingdom developments such as mastabas and coffin texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=6054" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:54</a> | Where does he think Adam and Eve lived? | He guessed southern Mesopotamia because of the rivers and regional markers in Eden traditions, while noting exact location is difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=6174" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:54</a> | Did the Hebrews have a base-ten system, and what about other ancient Near Eastern cultures? | He said Israelites and Egyptians used base ten, while Mesopotamians also used base sixty for mathematics and timekeeping. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=6302" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:02</a> | Are we living in the end times? | He said the New Testament treats the last days as beginning with the resurrection, but no one can identify the final consummation in advance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=6483" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:03</a> | Which Egyptian social class did the most innovative work, such as improving chariots? | He said innovation depended on the field; Hyksos-related groups adapted chariots, while physicians, scribes, engineers, and craftsmen innovated in different areas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=6704" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:44</a> | What kinds of books would have been held in Egyptian libraries around 1600 BC and later? | He said Egypt had scriptoria rather than public libraries, with contents depending on temple, palace, administrative, or religious function. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=6884" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:44</a> | In Revelation 14:14, does Jesus' sickle have an Old Testament connotation? | He said it is primarily an agricultural harvest metaphor, connected to the wheat and tares imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=7134" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:54</a> | What would he say to an atheist who says Christianity cannot be believed because the Bible was written by men long ago and changed over time? | He said age alone is not an argument against truth, and textual criticism shows the biblical message has remained remarkably stable despite minor changes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=7424" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:44</a> | What are his thoughts on C. S. Lewis' The Great Divorce? | He said he had not read it and could not comment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=7537" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:37</a> | Which political system is promoted as optimal in the Torah and Jewish sources? | He said Torah establishes a theocratic center for Israel but does not present monarchy or the judgeship as an optimal universal political model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=7754" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:14</a> | Why does the Israelite calendar seem to have more Babylonian than Egyptian influence? | He said calendar usage followed hegemony and exile, with later biblical collation reflecting Mesopotamian influence while Egyptian influence appears in some periods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=7893" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:33</a> | Are there theological differences between David and Kiara? | He said Kiara leans more Arminian while he leans more Calvinist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=7963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:43</a> | If there was no death before the fall, what was the purpose of the Tree of Life? | He said there was likely some kind of death before the fall, and the Tree of Life would have made human immortality permanent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=8174" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:14</a> | Why do many believe mankind existed before Adam and Eve, and could Eve from Adam's rib be metaphorical? | He said views differ widely, often depending on evolution and age-of-earth assumptions, and he did not take a firm position. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=8349" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:09</a> | How could business be conducted in Egypt without pharaonic involvement if Pharaoh sponsored major projects? | He said ordinary business operated through barter and private contracting; royal sponsorship was mainly for major royal or temple projects. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=8493" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:33</a> | Why was Sobekneferu hailed as a savior of Egypt when Egypt declined after her reign? | He said this was putting a brave face on a declining dynasty, especially in religious or official inscriptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=8714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:14</a> | Why does Egyptian art not use perspective, and why do temples seem lacking in ornamentation? | He said perspective was difficult in carved stone though sometimes attempted, and temples were originally highly painted and decorated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=8881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:01</a> | Does he have a favorite clear fulfilled prophecy written before the event? | He named Isaiah's prophecy of Cyrus, while cautioning that most prophecy is not primarily future prediction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=9138" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:18</a> | What does he think of Neoplatonism and its influence on Christian doctrine? | He said it influenced some background and church fathers, but more strongly fed Gnosticism than Christianity, and he does not think it works well theologically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=9326" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:26</a> | Did inequality between men and women in Egypt increase after Ramesses II? | He said he would frame it more as changing sponsorship and cultic emphasis than a simple decline in women's status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=9885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:45</a> | Why did rationalism or skepticism emerge in Middle Kingdom Egypt and then disappear by the 1100s? | He rejected the framing, saying the texts are better understood as religious idealization or speculation rather than atheistic rationalism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=10178" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:38</a> | Did evil exist before the fall if the serpent was already trying to lead Adam away from God? | He said evil begins with the serpent's iniquity and the fall narrative, not before it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=10361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:41</a> | Does Yahweh's name have to do with inhale and exhale? | He rejected that as an etymological argument with no merit, saying the name is tied to the verb "to be." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=10529" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:29</a> | Does Nicholas of Cusa rightly imply that Adam blamed God for introducing impurity into Eden through Eve? | He agreed Adam shifted blame onto both God and the woman, though he did not think that changed the judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=10721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:41</a> | What was the most philosophical text in Egyptian literature, and how was such literature distributed? | He pointed to the Book of Thoth and related teacher-student traditions, saying such works were copied and distributed on papyri. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=10947" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:27</a> | How many hours was the Egyptian work day, and was the average work day hard? | He said it was usually around ten hours, varied by job, and could be hard and dangerous for farmers or builders but easier for scribes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=11102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:02</a> | When early Exodus advocates say to trust calendar years, which calendar do they mean? | He said they usually mean a Julian-style solar year, which he considers an anachronistic standard for biblical interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=11204" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:44</a> | What suggestions does he have for those struggling with sin? | He recommended prayer, Bible reading, regular confession to God, short accounts, and accountability where needed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=11467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:07</a> | What are his thoughts on women pastors and elders? | He said the issue is complicated; he sees women pastors as appropriate in some ministries, but treats eldership and spiritual oversight as a distinct responsibility. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=12023" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:23</a> | How does Jesus reveal himself to people on the fence of faith or to non-believers? | He recommended reading the Gospels and Scripture, saying the Holy Spirit works through the text to form deep roots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=12277" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:37</a> | If evolution turned out to be true, would it conflict with humanity being made in the image of God? | He said no; the image of God concerns moral and spiritual capacity rather than biological mechanism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=13038" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:18</a> | Why does he lean toward Calvinism, and does Calvinism deny free will? | He said he leans toward total depravity but is not a five-point Calvinist, and that some Calvinist views allow free will while denying unaided movement toward God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=13151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:11</a> | Which verses would he use to refute a Jehovah's Witness? | He recommended Isaiah 44:6 because he reads it as presenting both the king of Israel and his Redeemer as Jehovah while affirming one God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=13450" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:10</a> | If the Solomonic temple is similar to many pagan temples, does that mean God can dwell in something similar to a pagan temple? | He said shared ancient Near Eastern temple forms do not matter because their meaning is redeemed and redirected to Yahweh worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=13722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:42</a> | How does he feel about Zahi Hawass' contributions to archaeology and Egyptology? | He said Hawass is a legitimate scholar, while also noting his political connectedness and telling a story about avoiding direct criticism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/MlFIL9cvfsg?t=13895" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:35</a> | When did Egypt's supposedly incestuous royal marriage practice end? | He said the premise is overstated; incestuous marriages were rare, often misread from kinship terms of endearment, and royal cases ended with the monarchy. |
